 {"id":"bd-2vr05.3.3","title":"task(scrollback): implement resize-safe viewport semantics and follow-output behavior","description":"Define deterministic behavior under width/height changes while preserving viewport intent.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Behavior in this task is deterministic for fixed inputs and capability profile.
- Required unit and/or E2E tests are added for the scoped behavior.
- Scope is complete only when dependencies remain satisfied and no regressions are introduced.

## Comprehensive Validation Hooks
- This task must add or update focused unit tests for core logic touched.
- This task must link to at least one end-to-end script path that validates integrated behavior.
- Validation output must include structured logs detailed enough for deterministic postmortem analysis.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Viewport and scrollback mappings are deterministic under navigation and resize.
- Follow-output behavior matches documented policy.
- Search across history is stable under continuous output.
- Performance/memory tests satisfy defined budgets.

## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for the implementation surface and edge-case regressions touched by this task.
- Deterministic end-to-end scripts are required for workflows materially affected by this task, and must run in CI.
- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (JSONL) with timestamps, correlation IDs, and failure context sufficient for deterministic replay and triage.","notes":"Execution Notes
- Viewport semantics should remain stable under continuous output and user scrolling.
- Follow-output state transitions must be explicit (user override versus automatic resume).
- Resize behavior must be policy-driven and documented (especially reflow or no-reflow decisions).
- Performance tests should include pathological histories, not just happy-path demos.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:41.252603119Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","updated_at":"2026-02-11T06:14:57.965708999Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T06:14:57.965686918Z","close_reason":"Completed resize-safe viewport semantics and explicit follow-output policy in frankenterm-web wasm/scroll with deterministic tests; validated by cargo fmt/check/clippy/test and wasm32 no-run; quick E2E emitted structured logs with 13/14 pass and pre-existing inline_basic assertion failure","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankenterm","resize","scrollback"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

- Validation output must include structured logs detailed enough for deterministic postmortem analysis.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Comprehensive unit tests are implemented for the scoped subsystem, including edge cases and regression vectors.

- Resize behavior must be policy-driven and documented (especially reflow or no-reflow decisions).
- Performance tests should include pathological histories, not just happy-path demos.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:41.991212688Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","updated_at":"2026-02-11T06:22:29.140336922Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T06:22:29.140317305Z","close_reason":"Completed large-scrollback stress, bounded render-window, and failure-injection tests in frankenterm-web wasm; validated by targeted fmt/check/clippy/test and wasm32 no-run compile; quick E2E produced structured logs with 13/14 pass and existing inline_basic assertion failure","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","frankenterm","scrollback"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.3.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.3.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.3.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.3.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-2vr05.4","title":"feature(frankenterm-js): selection, copy, and clipboard parity","description":"Background
- Selection and clipboard behavior are non-negotiable for terminal workflows (copy logs, commands, diagnostics).
- Edge cases around wraps, unicode, and scrolling are where many terminal emulators regress.

Goal
- Deliver resilient selection/copy/clipboard behavior with explicit policy and strong test coverage.

In Scope
- Rust-first selection state machine.
- Mouse and keyboard selection gestures.
- Extraction semantics for wrapped and unicode-rich content.
- Clipboard integration and paste-safety interactions.
- Stress testing under churn (output, scroll, resize).

Out of Scope
- Browser-specific one-off hacks that bypass core state model.

Why This Matters
- Reliable selection is a key adoption blocker for replacing xterm.js.

Acceptance Criteria
- Selection remains correct under heavy output and viewport changes.
- Copy output matches documented policy for wraps and graphemes.
- Clipboard flows are deterministic and test-backed.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Feature behavior is validated by comprehensive unit tests that cover core invariants and edge cases.
- Feature behavior is validated by end-to-end test scripts that exercise realistic user workflows.
- E2E runs emit detailed structured logs (JSONL or equivalent) sufficient for deterministic failure triage and replay.
- CI gates for this feature fail on regression in correctness, determinism, or declared standards scope.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:34.723118448Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","updated_at":"2026-02-24T07:18:39.893975581Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["clipboard","frankenterm","selection","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-2vr05.4.1","title":"task(selection): build rust-first selection state machine","description":"Linear/rectangular selection model with deterministic range normalization.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Behavior in this task is deterministic for fixed inputs and capability profile.
- Required unit and/or E2E tests are added for the scoped behavior.
- Scope is complete only when dependencies remain satisfied and no regressions are introduced.

## Comprehensive Validation Hooks
- This task must add or update focused unit tests for core logic touched.
- This task must link to at least one end-to-end script path that validates integrated behavior.
- Validation output must include structured logs detailed enough for deterministic postmortem analysis.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Selection behavior is correct for drag, keyboard extension, and viewport offsets.
- Extraction output matches policy for wraps and unicode graphemes.
- Clipboard integration paths are deterministic and test-covered.
- Stress tests under output churn pass without corruption.

## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for the implementation surface and edge-case regressions touched by this task.
- Deterministic end-to-end scripts are required for workflows materially affected by this task, and must run in CI.
- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (JSONL) with timestamps, correlation IDs, and failure context sufficient for deterministic replay and triage.","notes":"Execution Notes
- Selection logic should live in deterministic core state, not browser-only side effects.
- Ensure extraction rules are documented and fixture-backed for wraps, unicode, and trailing-space policy.
- Clipboard integration must have clear host callback boundaries and explicit fallback behavior.
- Stress cases must include simultaneous output churn and viewport movement.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:42.370104573Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","updated_at":"2026-02-14T22:53:09.679495093Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T22:53:09.679475296Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankenterm","selection","state-machine"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (JSONL) with timestamps, correlation IDs, and failure context sufficient for deterministic replay and triage.","notes":"2026-02-16: Added engine-backed extractSelectionText path in frankenterm-web/wasm.rs using frankenterm_core::Selection::extract_copy for unicode combining mark preservation + deterministic wide-cell handling; kept shadow-cell fallback; added two regression tests for engine path; validated via rch check/clippy/fmt and local wasm32 check/clippy/test --no-run (wasm tests not executable in this env without runner).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:43.123439294Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","updated_at":"2026-02-24T08:11:48.318002113Z","closed_at":"2026-02-24T08:11:48.317977246Z","close_reason":"Implemented copy extraction semantics: LineFlag enum (SoftWrap/HardNewline) per-row tracking, Grid.extract_text() with proper wrap joining, wide char continuation via WIDE_CONTINUATION sentinel, put_char_wide(), newline/carriage_return methods, trailing whitespace trimming. Fixed region scroll to shift line_flags. 30+ new tests, 259 total passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["copy","frankenterm","selection","unicode"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
